overview.goal: Replace replay-everything backward with a saved front-K visibility design.
overview.scope: This file is a structured engineering notebook, not hidden chain-of-thought.
overview.constraint: Keep the Torch autograd boundary and keep the graphics forward image path.

iteration.name: formula_audit
iteration.problem: The old replay kernel is both slow and locally wrong.
iteration.kernel_idea: Audit the scalar derivatives before touching the architecture.

bugs.bug: Old code used grad_color += grad_out * T. Correct form is grad_color += grad_out * T * alpha.
Old code negated mean gradients. Correct signs are positive because d(power)/d(mean) = [a*dx + b*dy, b*dx + c*dy].

pseudocode.step: for each hit: w = T * alpha

iteration.result: Keep replay only as an exact fallback, not as the primary backward.

iteration.name: saved_visibility_state
iteration.problem: Backward should not rediscover visibility from scratch on normal pixels.
iteration.kernel_idea: Capture the first K visible layers after forward sorting.

state_layout.tensor: Sorted gaussian indices, -1 for unused slots.
Captured contributor count, capped at K.

pseudocode.step: scan sorted gaussians front to back
if visible and count < K: store id/raw and increment count
else if visible and count == K: set overflow = 1 and break

iteration.result: Front-K backward can now run in O(pixels * K) for non-overflow pixels.

iteration.name: exact_common_path_and_exact_fallback
iteration.problem: Need exact gradients while keeping the common path cheap.

algorithm.step: Load ids/raw for n = front_count hits.
Build suffix colors S_i in registers with S_n = bg.
Walk layers front to back with prefix transmittance T.
Accumulate exact gradients to color, opacity, mean, and conic.

kernel.name: backward_overflow_replay

algorithm.step: Replay the full sorted list only for overflow pixels.
Use the exact forward image to recover the suffix color via behind = (out - A - w*c) / (T*(1-alpha)).
Apply the corrected local gradients from iteration 1.

kernel.reason: This keeps exactness even when the pixel has more than K contributors.

iteration.result: Exact gradients with a sparse expensive path instead of a universal expensive path.

iteration.name: validation_and_bringup
iteration.problem: Need a way to validate the math without claiming Apple-machine benchmark proof.
iteration.kernel_idea: Ship a reference Python implementation of the same algorithm.

validation.check: Reference image equals dense autograd reference.
Means, conics, colors, and opacities match dense autograd gradients on small cases.
Include both random and clamp/overflow-heavy scenes.

bringup_steps.step: Measure overflow ratio for K=2 and K=4 on real scenes.
Benchmark against v3 and v5 only after parity is clean.
